data.current_description: Get a single Dot Phrase.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a single Dot Phrase (EHR macro) by its unique identifier. Returns the complete Dot Phrase object including the trigger abbreviation and its expanded text definition. Requires authentication.
data.reasoning: The current description is minimal and lacks context about what a Dot Phrase is, what data is returned, and authentication requirements. The suggestion adds domain context (EHR macro reference), clarifies the return value structure, and specifies authentication needs for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Creates a Dot Phrase for the current user.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Dot Phrase (EHR macro) for the authenticated user. A Dot Phrase is a concise abbreviation (trigger) that expands into predefined extended text. Requires the trigger text and expansion content in the request. Returns the created Dot Phrase object with its unique identifier. The Dot Phrase is immediately available for use in the user's EHR interactions.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks specificity about what a Dot Phrase is, what inputs are required (trigger and expansion), what is returned, and when it becomes available. Added domain context referencing EHR macro terminology from the API schema definitions.

data.current_description: Retrieve a list of user's Dot Phrases.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all Dot Phrases (EHR macros) created by the authenticated user. Supports filtering and pagination through query parameters. Returns an array of Dot Phrase objects, each containing the trigger text, expansion content, and metadata. Use the next_cursor parameter for pagination through large result sets.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear. Description is too minimal and doesn't explain pagination support, filtering capabilities, or the structure of returned data. Added information about pagination, filtering, return structure, and how to navigate results using cursor-based pagination.

message: Operation 'jwt-logout' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details. data.current_description:
This endpoint will revoke and invalidate the specified user's session. 

Following a request to this endpoint, user will shortly need to obtain a new set of session tokens (access and refresh) from your server via the [Authenticate endpoint](/server/jwt-authenticate-user).

Note that this endpoint does not require authentication, so omit any authentication headers and only include your `refresh_token` in the request body.
data.description_suggestion: Revokes and invalidates the user's session by invalidating the provided refresh token. After logout, the user must re-authenticate via the Authenticate endpoint to obtain new session tokens. This endpoint does not require authentication headers - only include the `refresh_token` in the request body. Returns a success confirmation. The refresh token becomes immediately unusable.
data.reasoning: Description is mostly clear but lacks explicit information about the response format and the immediate effect of token invalidation. Adding clarity about what is returned and when the token becomes unusable helps AI agents understand the operation's complete behavior.

message: Operation 'copilot-listen-from-file' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary uses non-standard formatting with em-dash. data.current_description: Generate a transcript and a structured clinical note from an audio file. Only `audio/*` mime types are supported. The maximum duration is 10 minutes. If you have longer files, please use the [asynchronous equivalent](/server/copilot-listen-from-file-async).
data.summary_suggestion: Generate transcript and clinical note from audio file
data.description_suggestion: Generates a transcript and structured clinical note from an uploaded audio file in real-time. Accepts audio files with `audio/*` mime types up to 10 minutes in duration. Returns both the raw transcript and a formatted clinical note structured according to the specified note template and locale. For audio files longer than 10 minutes, use the asynchronous equivalent endpoint. Requires authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary uses non-standard formatting with em-dash. Description lacks details about what is returned (transcript format, note structure), required parameters (template, locale), and authentication requirements. AI agents need to understand the response structure and all required inputs to properly use this operation.

data.current_summary: Generate normalized data from a note
data.current_description:
Generate normalized data from a Nabla-generated clinical note by extracting ICD-10 and LOINC codes in a FHIR-compliant structure.

It adeptly identifies and categorizes key medical information within the notes, such as past medical history, family medical history, clinical assessments, and vital signs.

Each piece of medical information is represented as an array of FHIR's conditions/observations, each including relevant ICD-10 codes, thereby ensuring compatibility with standardized medical data frameworks.

The Normalize functionality is tailored to enhance the integration of clinical notes into Electronic Health Records (EHRs), facilitating accurate and efficient documentation by aligning with FHIR protocols.
data.summary_suggestion: Extract and normalize clinical data to FHIR format
data.description_suggestion: Extracts and normalizes clinical data from a clinical note into a FHIR-compliant structure. Accepts a clinical note as input and returns structured medical information including ICD-10 and LOINC codes organized as FHIR conditions and observations. Identifies and categorizes key clinical elements such as past medical history, family history, assessments, and vital signs. Output is compatible with Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems and FHIR protocols.
